definition.name: jordan-product
definition.definition: For elements a,b of an associative algebra (here B(H)), the (ambient) Jordan product is a∘b=(1/2)(ab+ba); it is bilinear, commutative (a∘b=b∘a), and keeps self-adjoint a,b inside B(H)_sa. Write a^2:=a∘a. The ambient ∘ on B(H)_sa satisfies the Jordan identity EXACTLY (HOS eq 2.18), the cancellation engine of the bridge. [def-jordan-product, HOS joa-m.md:812 eq 2.17]
